title: gladiolus taubertianus
intro: Gladiolus taubertianus, also known as the Tauber's gladiolus or Taubert's cornelian, is a plant species in the iris family that is native to the mountains of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Gladiolus taubertianus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Gladiolus taubertianus is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can also be used in herbal teas and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
